name: Glover
description_short: Glover Federal Credit Union is a small federal credit union in Honolulu, HI serving about 194 members in the manufacturing industry with $2.59M in assets.
description_long:
Glover Federal Credit Union was established in 1958 and has operated for 68 years as a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Honolulu, Hawaii. The institution is specifically designated to serve members of the manufacturing industry (All Other Manufacturing membership type) and maintains a single branch location at 248 Sand Island Access Rd in Honolulu. With approximately 194 members and $2.59 million in total assets as of December 2024, it operates as a smaller, specialized financial institution within Hawaii's credit union landscape.

The credit union offers standard banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/Debit Card programs, and loan products. Their loan portfolio consists primarily of other loans ($712,328) and unsecured loans ($19,605), indicating they provide member lending services. The institution maintains regular shares and deposits totaling $1,742,353 and reports positive net income of $39,907 for their most recent reporting period.

Glover Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself as an industry-specific credit union serving the manufacturing sector exclusively, which limits its membership but allows focused service to a particular community. The credit union is not a Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB) member and does not carry a low-income designation. Leadership includes CEO/Manager Michele Watanabe and Chairperson Demetrio Tolentino, with the institution subject to regular federal audits (last audited December 1, 2024).

As a very small credit union, Glover offers limited branch access (one location only) and serves a restricted membership base tied to manufacturing employment. Members should understand that this is a specialized, membership-based institution with minimal geographic convenience and limited service locations compared to larger banks or credit unions. The small asset base means fewer resources for digital banking infrastructure or extensive service offerings compared to larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federally chartered credit union with NCUSIF deposit insurance protection for member accounts
Industry-specific focus on manufacturing workers provides tailored service to a defined community
Established 68-year history demonstrates operational stability and longevity
Offers ATM/Debit Card programs for member convenience
Provides both secured and unsecured lending products to members
Maintains positive net income ($39,907) indicating financial health
Subject to regular federal audits ensuring regulatory compliance and transparency
cons: Extremely limited branch access with only 1 physical location in Honolulu
Very small membership base (194 members) limits network benefits and service resources
Manufacturing industry membership restriction means most consumers cannot join
Small asset base ($2.59M) limits product offerings and technological capabilities
No website listed, making online access and information retrieval difficult for members
best_for: Manufacturing industry employees in the Honolulu area with membership eligibility
Members seeking a community-focused credit union with a long operational history
Those already embedded in the manufacturing industry network who need basic banking services
diagnosis: Glover Federal Credit Union is best suited for manufacturing industry employees in the Honolulu area who already have membership eligibility and need basic banking services from a stable, established institution. The primary caveat is that this is an extremely small, membership-restricted credit union with only one branch location and no online presence, making it inaccessible to most consumers and limiting service convenience even for eligible members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
